$schema-doc: Allow-list for SDK Provider resource types registered in src/provisioning/register-providers.ts WITHOUT a corresponding integ fixture. Each entry is a deliberate verification gap with a documented rationale. The .claude/hooks/provider-integ-gate.sh hook accepts these without blocking commit. The scripts/build-integ-coverage-matrix.ts script surfaces them in the 'Allow-listed providers' section of docs/integ-coverage.md. Remove an entry when an integ fixture is added covering the type.
$why-sidecar: Stored in this sidecar (rather than as inline `// allow-no-integ:` comments on the register lines) so that allow-list edits do NOT touch src/provisioning/register-providers.ts. That file is in the integ-broad-gate cross-cutting scope; modifying it requires a real-AWS broad integ run (see .claude/hooks/integ-broad-gate.sh). The allow-list is metadata about coverage, not deploy-time behavior, so the gate trip is pure friction. This sidecar is in .claude/ which the gate does not scope.
AWS::ApiGateway::Account: Account-scoped singleton (CloudWatch role); apigateway integ explicitly opts out via cloudWatchRole: false to avoid mutating shared account state. Unit roundtrip + drift coverage suffice.
AWS::Glue::Workflow: drift-revert coverage only; no end-to-end Workflow integ yet (file follow-up if real-AWS Workflow lifecycle needs verification).
AWS::Glue::SecurityConfiguration: immutable resource type; unit roundtrip + drift coverage suffice. Add integ if a future bug surfaces a real-AWS-only behavior.
AWS::Glue::Job: drift coverage only; standalone Job integ deferred until a real-AWS issue surfaces (current usage at user sites is Studio-driven).
AWS::Glue::Crawler: drift coverage only; Crawler has its own start/stop schedule lifecycle, deferred until a user-reported regression makes integ ROI clear.
AWS::Glue::Connection: drift coverage only; Connection is a passive config object, no real-AWS lifecycle to exercise.
AWS::Glue::Trigger: drift coverage only; Trigger has DEACTIVATED-ACTIVATED state-machine semantics in update() that warrant a real-AWS integ — file follow-up.
AWS::EMR::InstanceFleetConfig: A cluster's instance-collection type is fixed at create (instance groups XOR instance fleets), so a single cost-bounded EMR cluster cannot exercise both InstanceGroupConfig and InstanceFleetConfig. The emr-instance-configs integ covers InstanceGroupConfig (the CDK-default group-based case); InstanceFleetConfig is structurally identical (same AddInstanceFleet create-poll / no-standalone-delete / best-effort-scale-to-0-for-TASK design, different API) and is covered by unit tests. Add a fleet-based integ if a real-AWS fleet-specific bug surfaces.
AWS::Kinesis::StreamConsumer: immutable type (every property change replaces); unit roundtrip suffices. Add integ if a Lambda EFO consumer pattern bug surfaces.
AWS::S3Tables::Namespace: existing s3-tables integ exercises only TableBucket. Namespace + Table are sub-resources sharing the same provider; unit roundtrip + parent-bucket integ suffice for now.
AWS::S3Tables::Table: see Namespace above. Extend tests/integration/s3-tables/ when a real-AWS Namespace/Table lifecycle bug surfaces.
AWS::RDS::DBProxy: rds-aurora integ does not currently provision DBProxy + TargetGroup + Endpoint (cost + 5-15 min create/delete). Unit roundtrip covers the diff matrix; extend rds-aurora when a real-AWS Proxy lifecycle bug warrants the integ time.
AWS::RDS::DBProxyTargetGroup: see DBProxy above — same rds-aurora-extend reasoning.
AWS::RDS::DBProxyEndpoint: see DBProxy above — same rds-aurora-extend reasoning.
AWS::ServiceDiscovery::Service: drift-revert-vpc covers PrivateDnsNamespace; Service is a sub-resource sharing the same provider. Add an ECS service-discovery integ if a Service-specific real-AWS bug surfaces.
